title: monarda punctata var. stanfieldii
intro: Monarda punctata var. stanfieldii, commonly known as spotted beebalm, is a type of flowering plant in the mint family. It is used in herbal medicine due to its antimicrobial, antiviral, and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as colds and flu. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has been traditionally used to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote overall health and well-being. Monarda punctata var. stanfieldii can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, making it a versatile and accessible herbal remedy.
